DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Josh D. Friedman, Esq. (510-844-5100; jfriedman@ccllp.law) of Collins + Collins LLP for Defendant Nibbi Bros. Associates, Inc.
Shounak Dharap, Esq. (ssd@arnslaw.com) and Jonathan Davis, Esq. (415.495.7800; jed@arnslaw.com) of The Arns Law Firm for Plaintiff.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC.'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Nibbi Bros. Associates, Inc.'s demurrer is overruled.

Workers' Compensation. Under Labor Code 3852, an employee may claim damages "against any person other than the employer." Nibbi is not pled to be plaintiff's employer. (Cmplt. 3:13-17.)

Duty of Care. Common law and Civil Code 1714 impose duties of care. Cal-OSHA regulations may establish duties in negligence actions against third parties. (Elsner v. Uveges (2004) 34 Cal.4th 915, 924.) Nibbi's role in controlling the construction site is not adjudicated at the pleading stage.

Damages. The complaint adequately pleads damages against Nibbi. (See, e.g., 1:22-24, 3:12-4:19, 7:21-26, 8:15-21, 10:1-16, Prayer 1:3-5.)

"Duplicative" Claims. The complaint's negligence and premises liability counts are not "identical" as Nibbi asserts. (Compare id. at 6:5-8:22 with 8:23-10:16.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Josh D. Friedman, Esq. (510-844-5100; jfriedman@ccllp.law) of Collins + Collins LLP for Defendant Nibbi Bros. Associates, Inc.
Shounak Dharap, Esq. (ssd@arnslaw.com) and Jonathan Davis, Esq. (415.495.7800; jed@arnslaw.com) of The Arns Law Firm for Plaintiff.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NIBBI BROS. ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Nibbi Bros. Associates, Inc.'s motion to strike is denied.

Premises Liability Count. The complaint's negligence and premises liability counts are not "identical" as Nibbi asserts. (Compare id. at 6:5-8:22 with 8:23-10:16.) In any event, a motion to strike a whole cause of action is improper. (Quiroz v. Seventh Ave. Center (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1281.)

Cal-OSHA Rules. Cal-OSHA regulations may establish duties in negligence actions against third parties. (Elsner v. Uveges (2004) 34 Cal.4th 915, 924.)

Damages. The complaint adequately pleads wage loss and personal property damages against Nibbi. (See, e.g., 1:22-24, 7:21-26, 8:15-21, 10:1-16, Prayer 1:3-5.)

Attorney Fees. The complaint seeks attorney fees "as allowed by law." This suffices at the pleading stage.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
